title: 第 58 節
en: III (17-19). On the duties of the shepherd's dog and the housedog.
IV (20-28). On the food due to the dog.
V (29-38). On the mad dog and the dog diseased; how they are to be kept, and cured.
VI (39-40). On the excellence of the dog.
VII (41-43). On the wolf-dog.
VIII (44-48). On the virtues and vices of the dog.
IX (49-50). Praise of the dog.
X (50-54). The water-dog.

This Fargard is the only complete fragment, still in existence, of a large canine literature: a whole section of the Ganb^-sar-ni^at Nask was dedicated to the dog (the so-called Fargard Pastbhaftrvastin; West, Dtnkard (Pahlavi Texts, IV), VIII, 23 ; 24, 5 ; 33» *c.)
la.
I. Which is the good creature among the creatures of the Good Spirit that from midnight till the sun is up goes and kills thousands of the creatures of the Evil Spirit?
2 (3). Ahura Mazda answered : ' The dog with the prickly back, with the long and thin muzzle, the 1 56 VENOiDAD.

dog Vanghdpara ^, which evil-speaking people call the DiL^aka * ; this is the good creature among the creatures of the Good Spirit that from midnight till the sun is up goes and kills thousands of the creatures of the Evil Spirit.

3 (6). ' And whosoever, O Zarathu^tra ! shall kill the dog with the prickly back, with the long and thin muzzle, the dog Vangh&para, which evil-speaking people call the Du-saka, kills his own *soul for nine generations, nor shall he find a way over the Kinvsicl bridge ^ unless he has, while alive, atoned for his sin */ 4 (lo). O Maker of the material world, thou Holy One ! If a man kill the dog with the prickly back, with the long and thin muzzle, the dog VanghApara, which evil-speaking people call the Du^ka, what is the penalty that he shall pay ?

^ The hedgehog. ' The hedgehog, according to the Bund. XIX, 28, is created in opposition to the ant that carries off grain, as it says that the hedgehog, every time that it voids urine into an ant's nest, will destroy a thousand ants ' (Bund. XIX, 28 ; cf. Saddar 57). When the Arabs conquered Saistan, the inhabitants submitted on the condition that hedgehogs should not be killed nor hunted for, as they got rid of the vipers which swarm in that country. Every house had its hedgehog (Yaqout, Dictionnaire de la Perse, p. 303). Plutarch counts the hedgehog amongst the animals sacred to the Magi (Quaestiones Conviviales, IV, 5, 2 :

Tnif£ d' dnb Zt^poaarpov ftayovt rift^p fiiP iv roir fjAkiara roy x!^fHraiQi9 ' Dusaka is the popular name of the hedgehog (Pers. 2U2ra). It is not >vithout importance which name is given to a being : 'When called by its high name, it is powerful' (Comm.); cf. § 6, and Farg. XVIII, 15.
' The bridge leading to Paradise ; see Farg. XIX, 30.
life even by performing a sacrifice to Sraosha* (cf. Farg. IX, 56, text and note).
FAROARD Xm. I57 Ahura Mazda answered : * A thousand stripes with the Aspahfi-aitra, a thousand stripes with the Sraosho-Z'arana.'
lb.
5 (13). Which is the evil creature among the creatures of the Evil Spirit that from midnight till the sun is up goes and kills thousands of the creatures of the Good Spirit ?

6(15), Ahura Mazda answered: * The da^va Zairimyangura \ which evil-speaking people call the Zairimy&ka^ this is the evil creature among the creatures of the Evil Spirit that from midnight till the sun is up goes and kills thousands of the creatures of the Good Spirit 7 (18). ' And whosoever, O Zarathu^tra ! shall kill the da^va Zairimyangura, which evil-speaking people call the Zairimy&ka, his sins in thought, word, and deed are redeemed as they would be by a Patet ; his sins in thought, word, and deed are atoned for ^ II.

8(21). 'Whosoever shall smite either a shepherd's dog, or a house-dog, or a Vohunazga dog *, or a trained dog*, his soul when passing to the other world, shall fly* howling louder and more sorely grieved than the sheep does in the lofty forest where the wolf ranges.
• The tortoise (Frdmjt and Riv&yats).
^ ' When not so called it is less strong ' (Corom.) Zairimjika is a lucky name, and means, as it seems, who lives in verdure ; Zairimyangura seems to mean 'the verdure-devourer.'
• Cf. Farg. XIV, 5. * See § 19, n. 2.
• A hunting-dog. • * From Paradise ' (Comm.)
1 58 VENDtDAD.

9 (24). ' No soul will come and meet his departing soul and help it, howling and grieved in the other world ; nor will the dogs that keep the [Alnvarf] bridge ^ help his departing soul howling and grieved in the other world.

10 (26). ' If a man shall smite a shepherd's dog so that it becomes unfit for work, if he shall cut off its ear or its paw, and thereupon a thief or a wolf break in and carry away [sheep] from the fold, without the dog giving any warning, the man shall pay for the loss, and he shall pay for the wound of the dog as for wilful wounding K 1 1 (31). ' If a man shall smite a house-dog so that It becomes unfit for work, if he shall cut off its ear or its paw, and thereupon a thief or a wolf break in and carry away [anything] from the house, without the dog giving any warning, the man shall pay for the loss, and he shall pay for the wound of the dog as for wilful wounding *.* 12 (36). O Maker of the material world, thou Holy One ! If a man shall smite a shepherd's dog, so that it gives up the ghost and the soul parts from the body, what is the penalty that he shall pay ?
zh: III（17-19）。論牧羊犬與家犬的職責。
IV（20-28）。論應當給予狗的食物。
V （29-38）。論瘋狗與患病之犬；其飼養與醫治之法。
VI（39-40）論狗之德行
VII（41–43）。論狼犬。
VIII（44-48）論犬之德與惡
IX（49-50）。對狗的讚頌。
這部法爾加德（Fargard）是現存僅有的完整片段，出自一部龐大的犬類文獻：《甘比薩尼亞特納斯克》（Ganb^-sar-ni^at Nask）的整個章節曾專門獻給狗（所謂的《帕斯特巴夫特瓦斯汀法爾加德》；韋斯特《丁卡德》（《巴列維文獻》第四卷），第八卷，第 23；24, 5；33 等）。

I'm ready to help you translate the English original text into Traditional Chinese following all the rules you've specified. However, I notice that the "英文原文" section only shows "la." which appears to be incomplete. Could you please provide the complete English text from Friedrich Max Müller's 1873 *Introduction to the Science of Religion* that you'd like me to translate?
我。哪一個是善靈的好生物，從午夜直到太陽升起都去殺死邪靈的成千上萬生物呢？
2 (3)。阿胡拉‧瑪茲達（Ahura Mazda）回答道：「具有豎起長毛的背脊，嘴吻修長而纖細的狗，
狗凡格德帕拉（Vanghdpara），邪惡之人稱之為迪拉卡（Dilâka）；這是善靈的造物中最良善者，自午夜至日出時分往來奔馳，殺死千萬邪靈的造物。
3 (6). '凡殺害長背多刺、長嘴細尖之犬，即彼犬 Vangh­para，惡言之人稱之為 Du-saka 者，瑣羅亞斯德啊！其人乃殺其自身之*靈魂九代，且彼人除非生前已為其罪贖罪，否則將無法越過 Kinvsicl 橋*。」4 (lo). 物質世界之造物主，聖潔者啊！若有人殺害長背多刺、長嘴細尖之犬，即彼犬 VanghApara，惡言之人稱之為 Du­ka，則其人應付何等罰罪？

刺蝟。根據《邦德經》第十九卷第二十八節，刺蝟被造來與搬運穀粒的螞蟻相對抗，經文載：「刺蝟每次向螞蟻窩裡排尿，就會摧毀一千隻螞蟻」（《邦德經》XIX, 28；參照《薩達爾》57）。阿拉伯人征服錫斯坦時，當地居民提出條件投降：不可殺害或獵捕刺蝟，因為刺蝟可以消滅在該地區滋生的蝰蛇。每戶人家都飼養刺蝟（雅庫特，《波斯辭典》，第303頁）。普魯塔克在其著作中將刺蝟列為馬吉人所尊崇的聖獸之一（《宴席問題集》IV, 5, 2：

我注意到您提供的「英文原文」實際上包含了損壞的文本、亂碼以及一些看似學術註釋的片段。該文本開頭是不可讀的符號，中間才出現可辨識的英文內容。 根據可讀部分，翻譯如下： Dusaka 是刺蝟的通俗名稱（波斯文 2U2ra）。給予一個生命體什麼名稱並非無關緊要：「當以其高名被呼喚時，它就具有力量」（註疏）；參照 § 6 及 Farg. XVIII, 15。 --- **說明**：原文的開頭部分為亂碼或損壞的文本，無法進行有意義的翻譯。若您能提供清晰的完整英文原文，我可以提供更準確、更完整的翻譯。
通往天堂的橋梁；見《法爾格迪經》(Farg.) XIX, 30。
生命，甚至透過向斯拉歐沙（Sraosha）*進行祭祀（參照《法爾格達德》第九卷，56節，正文與註釋）。
遠方 X 卷，第157節 阿胡拉‧馬茲達答道：「用阿斯帕赫－艾特拉（Aspahi-aitra）施以千鞭，用斯拉歐什－查拉納（Sraosho-Zarana）施以千鞭。」
我已準備好接收英文原文進行翻譯。請提供您要翻譯的《宗教學導論》英文原文內容。
5 (13). 邪靈的生物中，哪一種生物從午夜直到日出時，四處遊走並殺害善靈千千萬萬的生物？

6(15) 阿胡拉‧馬茲達答道：「那達瓦‧扎伊利姆揚古拉（dava Zairimyangura），惡言者稱之為扎伊利姆卡（Zairimy&ka），乃是惡靈之生物中最邪惡者，從午夜至日出之際，往來殺害善靈之千千萬萬生物 7(18)。凡殺此達瓦‧扎伊利姆揚古拉（即惡言者所稱之扎伊利姆卡）者，喔，查拉圖斯特拉！其思想、言語及行為之罪業，如同履行懺悔（Patet）般得以救贖；其思想、言語及行為之罪業均獲得贖罪。」II.
8(21)。「凡是擊打牧羊犬、家犬、沃胡納茲迦犬*或馴練犬*者，其靈魂在往生他世之時，其哀號之聲與悲慟之狀，將比狼出沒的高山林地中的羊群更為淒厲與痛楚。」
• 烏龜（《梵書》(Frdmjt) 與《禮儀經》(Riv&yats)）。
^ 「未如此稱呼時力量較弱」（《科羅姆經》）。Zairimjika 是一個吉祥的名字，其含義似乎是「住於蒼翠之中者」；Zairimyangura 似乎意為「蒼翠吞噬者」。
• 參見《法爾迦德》第十四章第五節。* 見第十九節註二。
• 一隻獵犬。• * 出自《天堂篇》（註釋本）

9 (24)。「沒有靈魂會前來迎接他離去的靈魂，也不會在另一個世界裡幫助它，使其在那裡哭號悲慟；同樣，看守[Alnvarf]橋的狗也不會在另一個世界裡幫助他離去的靈魂，使其在那裡哭號悲慟。

10 (26). 「若有人擊打牧羊犬以致其喪失勞動能力，若其割去其耳朵或其爪子，其後竊賊或狼群破門而入，自折疊處攫掠[羊隻]，犬未發警聲，則該人應賠償損失，並應如同故意傷害般賠償該犬之傷害。」 11 (31). 「若有人擊打看家犬以致其喪失勞動能力，若其割去其耳朵或其爪子，其後竊賊或狼群破門而入，自屋中攫掠[任何物品]，犬未發警聲，則該人應賠償損失，並應如同故意傷害般賠償該犬之傷害。」 12 (36). 嗟夫物質世界之造物主，聖哉聖哉！若有人擊打牧羊犬，以致其喪失生命，靈魂離體而去，則其應當承受之罰金為何？
